reasoning: The baseline for this forecast is the stock price on 2025-10-26. We are forecasting if the closing price on three future dates will be higher than this baseline. Over short durations, single stock returns have a base probability of being positive around 51-53% due to general market risk premiums. For Kimberly-Clark (KMB), the stock recently sold off significantly going into October, falling to its lowest point since February 2024 (~$120), while consensus price targets remain considerably higher (~$142). Given that expectations have been significantly lowered via multiple analyst target cuts ahead of the October 30 earnings call, a lot of bad news (shifted promotional activity, private-label competition, year-over-year profit decline) may already be priced in, offering a mildly asymmetric upside for a "relief rally" or a reversion to the mean. On the positive side, historical seasonality for KMB is strong in October-November (+5.89% average return). Given this setup, I estimate a slightly better-than-even chance of a positive price change over the very short term immediately following the earnings release. Following the explicit instructions that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, the probabilities for the later dates decay steadily toward 0.5.
